forecaster_note: A weak, mixed bag today and tomorrow — short-period E/ESE windswell tangled with a dying S line, 55–58kJ, nothing with real push. Saturday builds a breezy onshore on top of that, so don't bother waiting on it. SUNDAY is the swell of the week: a clean ENE pulse (7.2ft @ 11s from 74°) rebuilds energy to ~630kJ, sitting right in the prime 30–80° window that actually feeds these beaches. Long Reef and Curl Curl pick up the most size (4–5ft), Dee Why and Manly stay a cleaner 2–3ft. Wind's a light-moderate ESE onshore all day Sunday rather than the offshore we'd want, but the size and direction carry it regardless. — Surf Guru, read from Surfline model data (no written report scraped this run).

wind.summary: Glassy SW offshore at dawn (2–4km/h) swings cross-shore S through the morning (7–9km/h, gusts 14) — stays light all day, only nudging to 11km/h by afternoon. About as good as winter wind gets.

shark.summary: No shark detections anywhere from Palm Beach to Manly in the last 24h — drone flights running normally today. Last confirmed sighting was a drone spot at North Curl Curl, 8:56am Wed 15 Jul (now 45h+ old). Check the live map before you paddle out.

days.label: Today
days.note: A weak, disorganised mix today — short-period ESE windswell (2.9ft @ 6s) tangled with a dying S line (3.1ft @ 6s) and a barely-there ENE filler (1.3ft @ 8s), ~55kJ total. Wind is the saving grace: glassy SW offshore at dawn, swinging light cross-shore S by mid-morning and staying under 10km/h all session. No shark activity anywhere on the Northern Beaches in the last 24h. Fine for a wave count, nothing to chase.

swells.label: Right angle, barely any push — the best-direction swell is also the smallest
Good angle but short period — junky, breaks up fast
Dying S line, marginal angle — adds bump, not power

dee-why.window: Fair at best and patchy through the day (38/100 consistency) — the mixed short-period swells don't line up cleanly. Wind stays light all morning though, so it's pleasant even if unspectacular. Surf the dropping tide off the 10:27am high if you want the biggest push.

tip.label: Frontside rights, pick your peak
tip.text: Three weak swells fighting each other means the good sets are lottery-timing — sit slightly wider than usual and let the ENE/ESE combo stand one up rather than paddling for every bump. Everyday board, nothing more.

swells.label: Main energy source today — short period
Reef takes this closer to head-on

long-reef.window: Most consistent of the four today (100/100) even if the swell itself is unspectacular — the reef stays working all session on this mixed energy. Light onshore keeps it a bit textured rather than clean.

tip.label: Backside lefts
tip.text: Weak swell but the reef keeps standing something up all day here — good one if you just want wave count on the backhand without fighting Dee Why's crowd.

swells.label: Loads up here more than elsewhere

curl-curl.window: Decent consistency (62/100) for a weak day since Curl Curl eats the S component best of the four — still short-period and a bit onshore-bumpy though.

tip.label: Backside lefts
tip.text: The S line is small everywhere else but this is its best-fed spot — worth a look backside if Dee Why looks too disorganised to bother with.

manly.window: Smallest of the four (1–2ft) but the North Steyne corner shelters it into something tidy. Good backup if the others look like mush.

tip.label: Frontside, everyday board
tip.text: Nothing to get excited about — small and soft, but clean. Save the good board for Sunday.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: Similar weak mixed swell (~58kJ) but the wind turns against it — builds onshore/cross through the morning to 12–14km/h, gusting low 20s, and stays breezy all day. Long Reef, Curl Curl and Manly all slip to Poor–Fair. Dee Why just holds Fair. Not worth planning around — bank the energy for Sunday.

dee-why.window: Holds Fair better than the others (69/100 consistency) but the onshore builds through the day — if you're going, go early before the breeze fills in past 10am.

long-reef.window: Onshore chop kills most of the session (15/100 consistency) — skip unless you're desperate for a paddle.

curl-curl.window: Onshore all session, no clean windows today — skip.

manly.window: Small and cross-shore bumpy — not worth the drive tomorrow.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Saturday stays weak and breezy (see Tomorrow). SUNDAY is the session of the week — a clean ENE pulse (7.2ft @ 11s from 74°) rebuilds energy to ~630kJ, sitting right in the prime 30–80° window. Long Reef and Curl Curl pick up the most size (4–5ft), Dee Why and Manly a cleaner 2–3ft. Wind is a light-moderate ESE onshore all day (9–12km/h) rather than the offshore you'd want, but the size and direction carry it regardless. Values below are Sunday's — plan the session there, mid-morning as usual.

dee-why.window: Sunday mid-morning is the pick of the week — 74° ENE energy at 630kJ is right in your best window. Light ESE onshore adds a little texture but won't ruin it. Your session of the weekend.

long-reef.window: Picks up the most of Sunday's ENE pulse — 4–5ft and fully consistent all session. The size option if you want more than Dee Why offers.

curl-curl.window: Biggest of the four on Sunday but the reef gets closer to close-out at this size with the onshore chop — size over shape here.

manly.window: Clean-ish 2–3ft Sunday, fully consistent — the tidy backup to Dee Why if the carpark's full.
